title: How AI is powering greater efficiencies in business and commercial banking solutions

title: CFPB’s Supervision Division Releases New 'Humility Pledge'
text: Washington, D.C.—Today, the CFPB made changes to how the Supervision Division conducts exams. Previously, under the leadership of Director Chopra and Biden’s Director of Supervision Lorelei Salas, a former Soros activist who was put on leave in February 2025, this division was the weaponized arm of the CFPB.

title: CFPB Notifies Court it Cannot Lawfully Draw Funds from the Federal Reserve
text: Washington, D.C.—Today, the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) filed a notice informing the court in NTEU v. Vought that the Department of Justice’s Office of Legal Counsel (OLC) has determined that the Bureau may not legally request funds at this time from the Federal Reserve under Dodd-Frank.

title: CFPB Reaches Settlement with FirstCash, Inc. and Its Subsidiaries for Military Lending Act Violations

title: CFPB Amends Wise Order for Remittance Practices
text: The CFPB amended its January 30, 2025 consent order with the international remittance company Wise resolving claims including advertising inaccurate fees and failing to properly disclose exchange rates and other costs.

title: CFPB Announcement Regarding Enforcement Actions Related to Buy Now, Pay Later Loans
text: The CFPB is announcing that it will not prioritize enforcement actions taken on the basis of the Truth in Lending (Regulation Z); Use of Digital User Accounts to Access Buy Now, Pay Later Loans, 89 Fed. Reg. 47,068 (May 31, 2024) (“Buy Now, Pay Later”).

title: Robinhood customers can now let AI agents make trades and credit card purchases
text: Robinhood customers can now enable their AI agents to make payments and trade stocks on their behalf.

title: SBI Group invests in Temple Digital
text: Japanese financial behemoth SBI Holdings has led an investment round in Temple Digital Group, a New York-based outfit building trading infrastructure on the Canton Network blockchain. The size of the investment was not disclosed.

title: German media giant Bertelsmann scores banking licence
text: Riverty, the fintech startup from German media giant Bertelsmann, has gained an EU banking licence and is setting up shop as bank in Luxembourg.

title: HSBC emerging technology chief Glasner departs
text: Ian Glasner, HSBC's group head of emerging technology and innovation is quittng the bank after five years to return to California.

title: Indian travel-fintech Scapia raises $63m
text: Scapia, an Indian outfit that combines co-branded credit cards with travel booking, has raised $63 million in a funding round led by General Catalyst.

title: CommBank tests AI companion in banking app
text: CommBank is testing a new conversational AI interface within its banking app, designed to slice and dice spending and savings data to help customers to better manage their money

title: As rivals pull down the shutters, Nationwide to become UK's biggest branch network
text: With its rivals closing branches, Nationwide Building Society says its pledge to keep the doors open at all of its site means that it will have the UK's biggest network by June.

title: BNP Paribas partners Mistral to strengthen AI defences
text: BNP Paribas is working with French startup Mistral AI as it seeks to strengthen its cyber defences against weaknesses exposed by new models such as Anthropic's Mythos.

title: Circle co-founder raises $30m for agentic finance startup Catena
text: Agentic finance startup Catena Labs has raised $30 million in Series A financing and applied for a national trust bank charter.

title: Tether to build national stablecoin for Georgian Government
text: Tether has been commissioned by the Government of Georgia to develop GEL₮, a stablecoin representing the Georgian Lari.
